FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class ProgressRing

Namespace
FluentGpu.Controls
Assembly
FluentGpu.Controls.dll

A WinUI ProgressRing brought to 1:1 with controls/dev/ProgressRing. WinUI renders the ring via a Lottie AnimatedVisualPlayer (a continuous accent ring, NOT dots): a determinate variant whose accent arc's TrimEnd sweeps 0→1 over a (default-transparent) track ring, and an indeterminate variant whose accent arc rotates while its trim breathes. We reproduce both on the engine's SDF arc primitive (ArcSpec) — the determinate via a ProgressRing.ProgressRingTemplateSettings-computed sweep, the indeterminate via a rotation Keyframes loop at the exact WinUI cadence (900° per 2.0s, two cubic-bezier 0.167,0.167→0.833,0.833 half-segments).

Dimensional canon (ProgressRing.xaml / AnimatedVisuals/ProgressRingIndeterminate.cpp): Width=Height=32 default, MinWidth=MinHeight=16. Stroke is the Lottie weight, NOT the unused ProgressRingStrokeThickness=4 themeresource: the indeterminate ellipse uses StrokeThickness(1.5F) inside an 80px viewbox at Scale:5,5 (effective 1.5·5 = 7.5 in the viewbox), which maps to the 32px control as 7.5·32/80 = 3.0px (= size·0.09375). We scale it proportionally so larger rings keep the same visual weight. Foreground=AccentFillColorDefaultBrush (Tok.AccentDefault), Background=ControlFillColorTransparentBrush (transparent — the default track is invisible, exactly like WinUI). IsActive=false → the LayoutRoot fades to Opacity 0 (the Inactive visual state).

public static class ProgressRing

DefaultSize 

public const float DefaultSize = 32

Field Value

float

MinSize 

public const float MinSize = 16

Field Value

float

PartRing

The accent ring arc (the Lottie ellipse — determinate sweep / indeterminate spinner). The ArcSpec (color, stroke weight, sweep) is stock per-render computed styling a modifier sees and may override (b => b with { Arc = … }). Owned: OnRealized (the indeterminate trim-loop ref, chained with any modifier-supplied handler; the determinate arc owns nothing).

public const string PartRing = "Ring"

Field Value

string

Methods

Determinate(float, float, bool, ColorF?, ColorF?, TemplateParts?)

A determinate ring: the accent arc's sweep is value·360° from 12 o'clock clockwise, over a track ring. WinUI's default Background is ControlFillColorTransparentBrush, so the track is invisible unless a track color is supplied. foreground defaults to AccentFillColorDefault. parts = per-part styling keyed by PartRing (the accent arc).

public static BoxEl Determinate(float value, float size = 32, bool isActive = true, ColorF? foreground = null, ColorF? track = null, TemplateParts? parts = null)

Parameters

value float
size float
isActive bool
foreground ColorF?
track ColorF?
parts TemplateParts

Returns

BoxEl

Indeterminate(float, bool, ColorF?, TemplateParts?)

An indeterminate spinner: a round-capped accent arc rotating continuously at the WinUI cadence (900° per 2.0s with the Lottie cubic-bezier spline). isActive=false fades it out (Inactive). parts = per-part styling keyed by PartRing (the spinning arc).

public static Element Indeterminate(float size = 32, bool isActive = true, ColorF? foreground = null, TemplateParts? parts = null)

Parameters

size float
isActive bool
foreground ColorF?
parts TemplateParts

Returns

Element
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
